Title: Add a ListBlocks / ListItems tool for in-game block discovery
Description:
The agent cannot currently discover what blocks or items exist in the loaded game world/mods. This causes gather loops to spin on hardcoded block names that don't exist in the biome or on modded servers.

Proposed solution: add a 'ListBlocks' tool that queries Mineflayer's block registry and returns all registered block names (optionally filtered by a prefix or tag).

Implementation:
1. Add a 'listBlocks' case to MineflayerAdapter/index.js:
   - args: { filter?: string } — optional name prefix/substring to narrow results
   - Response: sendEvent('blockList', { blocks: [...] }) with all matching names from bot.registry.blocksByName
2. Add a corresponding WebSocketBridge parse case for 'blockList' event.
3. Add ListBlocksTool.cs in Agent.Tools/Tools/:
   - Name: 'ListBlocks'
   - ExecuteAsync sends the 'listBlocks' action and returns the block list in ToolResult.Data
4. Register in Program.cs ToolDispatcher.
5. Wire into chat: user can ask 'what wood is in this biome?' and the LLM (or planner) calls ListBlocks.

Stretch: add a 'scanNearby' action that does a local block census within N blocks of the bot and returns a frequency-ranked list. This is more useful for immediate planning (e.g. 'I see 12 spruce_log, 0 oak_log nearby — switch to spruce_log goal').

This is the primary mechanism for mod support: modded blocks show up in bot.registry.blocksByName and in a local scan, so the agent can learn new block IDs without any hardcoded lists.
